name: Polenta Pasticciata- Polenta Lasagne
recipeIngredient: 1/3 oz Dried porcini or other wild mushrooms
1 lb Lean ground beef or 1/2 lb flank steak
35 oz Can plum tomatoes, drained/canned juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour polenta onto an oiled work surface and spread to a thickness of about 1/4 inch. Let cool completely and firm, about 15 minutes. Cut into 3-inch squares; set aside. (Transfer squares to a baking sheet, cover, and refrigerate overnight.)
2. For the sauce: Soak dried mushrooms in 1/3 cup warm water until softened, about 30 minutes. Strain liquid through a fine sieve; reserve. Chop mushrooms coarsely.
3. Heat oil and butter in a large skillet. Add onions, parsley, carrots, and celery; sauté until softened, about 10 minutes. Add ground meat; sauté until lightly browned, about 8 minutes. Stir in mushrooms, reserved mushroom soaking liquid, tomato paste, and wine; simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Add tomatoes and their reserved juice; partially cover and simmer until sauce thickens slightly, about 30 minutes. Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste. (Cool, cover, and refrigerate overnight if desired.)
5. Preheat oven to 450°F. Arrange half of the polenta squares in the bottom of an oiled 13x9-inch baking pan. Pour half of the sauce over the polenta squares; spread to cover. Sprinkle half of the grated cheese over the sauce. Repeat with another layer of each.
6. Bake until lasagne is heated through and cheese is golden brown, about 15-20 minutes. Let stand for 10 minutes before cutting into squares and serving.
